shortTitle: You're the worlds most powerful mafia boss

content.text: You snap awake, heart hammering in your chest, sweat slicking your palms. The city glows in the distance, just past the shield of inch-thick bulletproof glass.
content.imageDescription: Close-up of a person jolting awake in bed, sweat visible on their face and hands. Through a wide, bulletproof glass window behind them, an urban skyline glows at night with city lights.

content.text: Down below: traffic never sleeps, but neither do the shadows, whispers, and silent intent that fill every alley, every penthouse and empty hallway of your empire. This is your kingdom—a world built on loyalty and bullets, on secrets buried deeper than the river. But you know it only takes a flicker, an instant. Everything can turn to smoke.
content.imageDescription: Bird’s-eye view looking down from a penthouse window at busy city streets filled with cars. Shadows creep through narrow alleys, a sense of hidden dangers everywhere. The scene feels both regal and threatening, hinting at untold secrets and looming menace.

content.text: You blink away exhaustion. Smell the expensive cologne and the coppery tang of dread that lingers after another near-miss. Every luxury here—crystal decanters, velvet carpets, modern art—disguises a threat. You don’t remember when you last slept without a gun under your pillow. Maybe you never did.
content.imageDescription: Interior of a lavish penthouse: plush velvet carpets, designer furniture, crystal decanters on a sideboard, abstract modern art on the walls. In the foreground, a handgun partially hidden under a pillow on the bed.

content.text: Your phone vibrates. Unknown number. You hesitate, then swipe. Silence, then a breath. Then, in a voice you recognize: “We have a problem. The warehouse in the docks. Someone flipped.” No names. Never names. The line goes dead.
content.imageDescription: Close-up of a hand picking up a vibrating smartphone showing 'Unknown Number.' The caller’s message is heard as a voiceover. The character’s tense expression is illuminated by the phone screen in a dim room.

content.text: You reach for your revolver. Thumb the safety. You force yourself upright. You’re not just awake; you’re alone at the center of an empire spinning faster every night, too many knives circling, too many debts crawling up the walls. Even your allies eye you sideways, wondering if tonight’s the night you fall.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist, sitting up in bed, checks a revolver before standing. In the shadows of the modern room, ghostly silhouettes of potential threats and treacherous allies surround, eyeing them suspiciously.

content.text: Your reflection in black glass doesn’t blink. Old scars itch beneath the suit.
content.imageDescription: A dramatic over-the-shoulder shot: the main character in a sleek suit staring at their own stern reflection in a large, dark-tinted window. Faded scars are visible at the collar.

content.text: You walk toward your private lift—bodyguards on edge, guns half-drawn, ears pricked for trouble. A tremor in the air. A whisper, a window left open, two seconds late on a security check. Paranoia? Or survival? Maybe same thing.
content.imageDescription: Wide shot: the main character strides towards a private elevator, flanked by tense bodyguards with hands near their weapons. A curtain flutters from an open window, heightening the sense of unease.

content.text: You remind yourself to trust no one—certainly not the man watching the elevator doors close, his hand just a bit too close to his jacket. Maybe he’s hungry for your job. Maybe he’s bought. Maybe you’ll never know.
content.imageDescription: Inside the elevator: a suspicious bodyguard stands too close, hand hovering near his jacket. The protagonist watches him warily as the doors slide closed, tension thick in the small space.

content.text: The city throbs all around you. Rain collects against the windows. Underneath the rhythm, a second, harder song: the memory of old friends who vanished, of luck turning like a switchblade. Did power ever feel good, or just numb?
content.imageDescription: Rain streaks down the penthouse windows. The city’s vibrant lights pulse in the wet, reflecting a somber mood. In the protagonist’s eyes, a distant look hints at loss and disillusionment.

content.text: The elevator shudders to a stop. Another text. Three code words. Drop location. You check the gun again. Weight and balance. You step out into a corridor, marble echoing under your feet, smell of disinfectant barely masking spilled secrets.
content.imageDescription: Shot of the elevator doors opening to a sleek marble corridor. The protagonist reads a cryptic text, then checks the revolver’s weight in their hand before stepping out. The space is sterile but ominous.

content.text: Your consigliere waits, ice-eyed and unreadable. “There’s been movement,” he murmurs. "Cops. Some of ours. The ones on your payroll, and some you just pay to look away. They’re circling. And there’s talk: someone in the family has been taking fat stacks for information. Meeting tonight at the augury club. You want me there?"
content.imageDescription: A well-dressed, sharp-eyed consigliere waits in the hallway. He speaks quietly to the protagonist, his demeanor unreadable. The corridor intensifies the charged atmosphere between them.

content.text: You say yes, but you’re thinking: can you trust even him? The longer you sit on this throne, the shorter your days feel. Alliances are shifting sand. Blood ties have a price tag. One wrong move, and your kingdom splinters overnight.
content.imageDescription: Tight shot on the protagonist’s contemplative face, the consigliere just out of focus behind. Imagery of shifting sands and fragmented family portraits flicker ghost-like around them, symbolizing fragile alliances.

content.text: You plan your route, watching for tails, scanning faces, every window a possible muzzle flash. You ditch the car. Walk two blocks under the neon haze, the street slick with last night’s rain. A beggar eyes you—a ghost of your once-hungry self. You don’t stop. You can’t afford nostalgia. Take a left.
content.imageDescription: Montage: the protagonist maneuvers through city streets, ditching a car for a nighttime walk under neon signs reflected on wet pavement. A ragged beggar locks eyes with them briefly, stirring a hint of old memories.

content.text: Down an alley, through an unmarked steel door. Inside, soft red lights, smoke, laughter sharp enough to cut. Your inner circle waits in a back booth, all false smiles and stiff drinks. The air is thick with tension. You nod. Watch their hands. Know that even family can turn overnight. Especially when there’s more money on the table than loyalty ever bought.
content.imageDescription: Dirty urban alley leads to an anonymous steel door. Inside, a smoky, dim-lit club with red lighting and a private back booth. The inner circle waits: tense, watching each other, suspicious hands near drinks. Laughter barely hides unease.

content.text: The drinks come. You don’t touch yours. Not yet. Laughter dies. Silence fills the cracks. The first betrayal always comes soft—a casual comment, a hesitation too long, a sideways glance at who’s not here. You ask about the docks. Answers come too quick. Too smooth. You file their words away, build a map of who’s safe, who’s useful, who’ll be dead in a week.
content.imageDescription: Club booth, group of well-dressed associates. The protagonist refuses to drink, listening as casual small talk shifts to tense silence. Subtle glances and suspicious pauses reveal underlying betrayals.

content.text: Someone else joins—eyes rimmed red, expensive watch—a reminder that wealth is armor, but only until someone pries it off your wrist. Reports from the street filter in. One stash house torched, two enforcers missing, bodies dumped behind your own club. The message is clear: someone’s coming for the throne, already carving their initials into your empire’s flesh.
content.imageDescription: A new figure with bloodshot eyes and a flashy watch slides into the booth. The protagonist receives whispered updates: news of arson, missing men, and bodies found behind their club. Tension and threat hang heavy in the room.

content.text: Your consigliere slips you a note: a meeting with the Feds, hush money demand double last month. They want you to drop your own man to buy peace. Triangle of pressure. Cops, rivals, your own blood. Someone just set fire to your future, and you’re the only one with a fire extinguisher. Or a gas can.
content.imageDescription: Close-up: the consigliere discreetly hands a folded note under the table. The protagonist scans it, face hardening. Visual metaphors: burning paper, a fire extinguisher and a gas can symbolizing his stark choices.

content.text: For a half second, you wonder: walk away? Take the money and vanish, slip through all the cracks you spent your life patching? The thought vanishes. You’ve built too much. Or maybe you’re chained to it now. The world outside won’t let a monster like you hide in sunlight.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist sits apart from the group in a shadowy corner, vision blurring into daydream: flashes of peace and escape evaporate around them, replaced by metaphorical chains binding them to their empire.

content.text: Backroom deals heat up. You sit down with an enemy—old grudges silenced by common threat. The handshake is cold. You talk terms. You promise less than you deliver, always holding the ace. This city runs on favors, not laws. You trade secrets for time, money for silence, your own men for one more sunrise.
content.imageDescription: A secretive backroom with two adversaries facing each other across a table, hands locked in a tense handshake. Documents, cash, and subtle signaling play between them. Shadows obscure their faces, reflecting mistrust.

content.text: Suddenly, shots outside. Glass explodes inward, a bodyguard’s scream—chaos. You hit the floor, steel in hand, adrenaline roaring. You return fire, a flash, then darkness. You survive by luck and muscle memory. When the dust settles, two dead, three missing. The message: you’re not safe, not anywhere, not with anyone. Trust isn’t just risk—it’s suicide.
content.imageDescription: Action scene: bullets shatter the club’s windows, panicked bodyguards dive as the protagonist hits the floor, firing back. After the dust settles, bodies and chaos fill the scene—shattered glass everywhere, an atmosphere of raw danger.

content.text: You climb back into your armored SUV, mind racing, sweat cold on your back. A call from your lieutenants: someone’s flipping, another is gunning for your seat. You issue orders with an iron voice, knowing tonight, it’s either them or you. No quarter given. No forgiveness left in the tank. You’re both the judge and the executioner now.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist, face grim and strained, sits alone in an armored SUV. On a phone call, they issue hard, decisive orders. The city’s night lights blur past the bulletproof windows, underscoring urgency and ruthlessness.

content.text: Back to the penthouse. Windows still unbroken, city still breathing. But sleep is a stranger; only ghosts and gold-plated pistols to keep you company. You stare at your own reflection until it blurs, wondering how many of your choices were really yours—how many were made by hunger, threat, the cold logic of survival. If you keep surviving, what’s left to save?
content.imageDescription: Back in the penthouse, the protagonist sits on the edge of a luxurious bed with a gold-plated pistol nearby. They gaze into their distorted reflection in a window, haunted by doubt and weariness.

content.text: You hear footsteps in the hall, keys in the lock. Every muscle tenses. Not tonight, you think. Not here. Not yet. But the fear is never gone. You sip untouched whiskey, taste the poison under the burn. Even if you win, you lose yourself. That’s the price no one sees—the loneliness, the paranoia, the hush of footsteps echoing into your bones. The knowledge that tomorrow, you’ll do it all again, and one night, the knock on the door won’t be theirs—it’ll be yours.
content.imageDescription: Nighttime: the protagonist sits alone in the shadowy penthouse, a glass of whiskey nearly untouched in their hand. The silhouette of someone turning a key in the door is visible in the background while the protagonist tenses, embodying dread and relentless paranoia.

protagonist.description: The protagonist, sitting up in bed, checks a revolver before standing.

stranger.description: But sleep is a stranger; only ghosts and gold-plated pistols to keep you company.

warehouse.description: Setting (warehouse): The warehouse in the docks.

room.description: Setting (room): In the shadows of the modern room, ghostly silhouettes of potential threats and treacherous allies surround, eyeing them suspiciously.

alley.description: Setting (alley): Dirty urban alley leads to an anonymous steel door. Down an alley, through an unmarked steel door.

house.description: Setting (house): Nighttime: the protagonist sits alone in the shadowy penthouse, a glass of whiskey nearly untouched in their hand.
